acceptedAnswer.text: Taiwan Stock Radar is an open-source Taiwan equity analysis skill for AI agents, analysts, and developers. In v1.2 it focuses on one stock at a time and runs a multi-agent research council instead of a generic stock summary.

mainEntity.name: How does the multi-agent council work?

acceptedAnswer.text: The skill assigns specialist AI roles such as technical, chip-flow, fundamental, catalyst, and risk analysis. Each role produces an independent view, the chair agent moderates disagreement, and the final output is synthesized through weighted judgment.

mainEntity.name: Why use different AI personalities or specialist roles?

acceptedAnswer.text: Different specialist roles reduce single-view bias. They make the final result more rigorous by separating structure, flow, business quality, timing, and risk instead of collapsing everything into one voice.

mainEntity.name: What does the final output include?

acceptedAnswer.text: A standard v1.2 output includes the final thesis, base/bull/bear scenarios, preferred buy zone, entry triggers, stop-loss, take-profit ladder, invalidation, confidence, and visible dissent notes from the council.

mainEntity.name: Does Taiwan Stock Radar guarantee stock performance?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Taiwan Stock Radar is a research and decision-support skill. It provides structured judgment and risk-aware planning, not certainty about future returns.
